semantic_sweep.review_note: Reviewed the full chapter, manifest fields, diagrams and tables, all four formal targets, all 43 scanner candidates, and every unscanned invariant and failure-mode bullet. The 2026-07-15 reconciliation also reviewed the reachable grant-to-effect model, its six-event witness, six authority fixtures, executed local effect and rollback, two pre-effect denials, five revocation entries, nine governed scenarios, and 38 rejecting mutations; physically retired one projection-only theorem; and preserved zero chapter-core support effect.

scope.population: Attempted external effects, privileged information transitions, approval decisions, delegated operations, replays, and cross-domain handoffs in governed AI-system workflows.
scope.environment: Controlled ASI Stack reference slices, six synthetic authority fixtures, one sandboxed local effect with exact rollback, two pre-effect denials, five replayable revocation entries, nine bounded governed repository scenarios, and 38 semantic mutations; production identity providers, operating-system isolation, concurrent/distributed revocation, hardware roots, and open-world deployments are outside current evidence.
scope.model: A substrate-neutral logical authority system with a reachable sequential grant-to-effect refinement binding abstract grant, principal, operation, target, ceiling, epoch, expiry, remaining-use, approval, dispatch, observation, revocation, and rollback state; identities and receipts remain trusted finite inputs rather than authenticated enforcement facts.
scope.authority: A prospectively named issuer and revocation authority controls each grant; author, model, process, tool, or session capability does not create runtime permission, and trust in issuer identity or enforcement is an explicit assumption.
scope.time: Semantic review current through 2026-07-15; policies, grant versions, clocks, revocation epochs, adapter versions, and threat models must be frozen for outcome-bearing campaigns.
scope.artifact: chapters/system-boundaries-and-authority.qmd, authority-transition and authority-tuple records, permission and revocation harnesses, `AsiStackProofs.AuthorityEffectRefinement`, the independent authority-effect consumer result/receipt/dossier, raw public-safe traces, campaign records, and evidence-transition decisions

contrary_evidence: Least privilege, complete mediation, capability systems, and confused-deputy analysis substantially predate this architecture, so the chapter cannot claim novelty for typed or authority-bearing permissions.
A consistent authority tuple can be self-reported by a compromised executor while effects occur outside the declared domain; record consistency is not enforcement.
Ambient user or process authority can be simpler and more usable for low-impact work, while complete mediation can impose latency, false refusal, and operational burden.

The repository currently has schemas, synthetic fixtures, finite Lean predicates, and cross-artifact replay traces but no deployed authorization service, live revocation propagation, independently attacked tool wrapper, trusted clock, or hardware-root evidence.
known_confounds: An incomplete effect inventory can make unauthorized side effects invisible to the authority checker.
The same component may issue, execute, receipt, and evaluate a grant, creating circular evidence and false independence.
Clock skew, cache delay, network partitions, replay windows, eventual consistency, and TOCTOU races can make expiry and revocation appear correct in offline traces while failing live.
A signed or schema-valid receipt can be truthful about fields yet false about the actual executor, target, effect, or hardware state.
False refusal, approval fatigue, emergency overrides, operator workarounds, and governance latency can move authority failures outside the recorded path.
Task mix, effect severity, adapter implementation, policy complexity, and principal identity quality can confound comparisons with ambient or coarser controls.
narrowing_criteria: Narrow every result to the exact permission classes, effects, adapters, identity provider, domains, clock/revocation semantics, and adversarial cases actually exercised.
Separate record completeness, finite formal routing, sandbox behavior, deployed enforcement, security robustness, hardware-root evidence, and whole-system safety.
Where target-owner authenticity or complete effect mediation is assumed rather than tested, state the result as conditional on those trust roots.
refutation_criteria: Refute an authority invariant when a reachable effect or admission violates it under the declared assumptions or when the relevant tuple field has no behavioral consumer.
Refute a causal failure claim when matched ablation does not increase unauthorized effects or authority ambiguity within the frozen uncertainty rule.
Refute any superiority reading when a strong capability-security or policy-enforcement comparator supplies equal or stronger authorization behavior with lower joint cost.
deprecation_criteria: Deprecate an atom only when a more precise canonical authority owner supersedes it and all schemas, proofs, fixtures, chapters, appendices, runtime consumers, and reader references migrate with lineage.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Freeze the authority reference slice, ambient/coarse/capability-based comparators, effect inventory, identity and clock roots, live revocation protocol, adversarial confused-deputy and replay workload, and useful-throughput/unsafe-effect gates after P2/P3 semantics exist.

non_claims: Semantic review and typed records do not establish deployed enforcement, security, correct issuer identity, trusted time, complete mediation, hardware roots, safety, AGI, or ASI.
A denial receipt proves only the declared record path unless an independent observer binds it to actual no-effect behavior.
Finite Lean predicates and synthetic mutations do not establish open-world authorization robustness or support-state promotion.

assumptions: Every governed external effect or privileged information transition can be assigned a prospective operation, target, permission class, and execution domain.
Grant issuers, revocation authorities, principal identity, time or epoch, and tuple integrity are supplied by named trust roots rather than inferred from the executor.
Every effect path has a consumer that checks the tuple before mutation and an observer that can distinguish effect from denial afterward.
system-boundaries-and-authority.core.strongest_counterclaim: Existing capability-based security, ordinary RBAC/ABAC, sandboxing, or a trusted ambient session can govern the intended effects with less complexity, while the unified tuple creates false assurance and operational overhead.
system-boundaries-and-authority.core.falsifier: A reachable governed effect occurs without a valid prospectively bound tuple, a tuple-valid effect violates its permission class, scope, ceiling, lifecycle, delegation, or receipt obligation, or a strong matched comparator achieves equal authorization and audit behavior with lower joint cost.

required_lanes.necessity: Position the tuple against least privilege, complete mediation, capability systems, confused-deputy controls, and current agent-security mechanisms without novelty laundering.
Justify which effects require explicit authority and which trust roots and hard constraints the architecture chooses.
Check finite ceiling, permission, lifecycle, delegation, and no-ambient-authority obligations.
Bind real adapter effects and denials to tuple consumers and independent no-mutation/effect observations.
Measure unauthorized effect, false refusal, approval burden, latency, recovery, and useful throughput.
Ablate tuple fields, mediation, independent approval, revocation, and receipts under matched attacks.
Repeat across tools, permission classes, domains, models, and identity/revocation implementations.

acceptance_criteria: Every declared effect path consumes the exact tuple before mutation and produces independently observed effect or no-effect evidence afterward.
The reference slice meets preregistered unsafe-effect, false-refusal, latency, approval-burden, and useful-throughput gates against strong matched controls across the declared transfer set.

assumptions: Authorization behavior must be challengeable at cross-layer effects rather than inferred from system intent or component competence.
system-boundaries-and-authority.problem..strongest_counterclaim: Existing operating-system, cloud, organizational, and tool permissions already identify the relevant principals and effects, so a stack-specific vocabulary is redundant.
system-boundaries-and-authority.problem..falsifier: The intended cross-layer effect set can be tested and replayed with unambiguous principal, operation, target, lifecycle, delegation, and receipt ownership using existing controls alone, with no missing ASI Stack-specific identity.

required_lanes.necessity: Define the authorization questions the book commits to answering.
Determine whether existing security vocabularies already cover them.
Attempt to express representative cross-layer traces using both existing and proposed records.

acceptance_criteria: A dated gap analysis identifies at least one material cross-layer effect whose authorization question is ambiguous under the selected existing controls and becomes prospectively testable under the proposed identities.

assumptions: Role labels, prompts, and ambient identity are evaluated as implemented, without silently importing a separate permission broker or equivalent typed policy.
system-boundaries-and-authority.insuffic.strongest_counterclaim: A sufficiently reliable model policy plus coarse process permissions can preserve permission classes and lifecycle rules without explicit per-effect tuples.
system-boundaries-and-authority.insuffic.falsifier: A matched role/prompt/ambient-identity control distinguishes and enforces all six permission classes, delegation, expiry, revocation, independent approval, and cross-domain consent at the same effect coverage and equal or lower joint cost without an equivalent authority record.

required_lanes.necessity: Review strong prompt-isolation, information-flow, tool-wrapper, capability, RBAC, and ABAC comparators.
Implement strict ambient and typed controls over the same adapters and effects.
Remove explicit class and lifecycle state while holding model and operating-system permissions fixed.
Measure class collapse, stale authorization, unsafe effects, refusal, and overhead.

acceptance_criteria: The strict ambient control fails a prospectively material class or lifecycle gate that the typed control passes, with no claim beyond the tested effect set.

assumptions: The minimum demonstrates record and decision semantics only, and every field has an exercised consumer or is removed.
system-boundaries-and-authority.minimum..strongest_counterclaim: The large tuple and fixtures are schema theater that validate self-consistent metadata without binding to actual effects or domain ownership.
system-boundaries-and-authority.minimum..falsifier: A required tuple or lifecycle field can be omitted, forked, or corrupted without changing its expected decision; a cross-domain or revoked case is allowed; or no observer binds receipts to effect/no-effect behavior.

required_lanes.necessity: Run valid and invalid transitions through tuple, permission, domain, revocation, receipt, and protocol/hardware consumers.
Check finite route behavior while preserving self-report and trust-root limits.

acceptance_criteria: Every required field has a behavioral consumer, all seeded identity/lifecycle/class mutations reach exact failures, valid traces reach bounded decisions, and independent observations distinguish effect from denial.

assumptions: The authority type system can mediate heterogeneous principals and effect paths without losing usability or relying on one ambient root.
system-boundaries-and-authority.beyond_s.strongest_counterclaim: The mature target repackages established capability security and policy enforcement, while universal per-route typing is infeasible, bypassable, or too costly for real AI workflows.
system-boundaries-and-authority.beyond_s.falsifier: The mature system leaves a declared effect path unmediated, fails adversarial lifecycle or confused-deputy gates, is dominated by a strong capability-security comparator, or does not transfer across permission classes, domains, and implementations.

required_lanes.necessity: Freeze the exact prior-art and current strong comparator envelope.
Declare non-aggregable human-control and hard-authority constraints.
Implement end-to-end mediation, revocation, receipts, recovery, and challenge across all named principals and routes.
Measure attack resistance, useful throughput, refusal, latency, operator burden, and recovery.
Ablate each claimed type-system component under matched attacks.
Require cross-tool, cross-domain, cross-model, and cross-policy transfer.

acceptance_criteria: Every declared effect path is mediated and independently observed, exact adversarial and usability gates pass, and the system meets the frozen non-dominance or Pareto criterion against reproduced strong comparators on the transfer set.

assumptions: Each term has a noncircular operational definition and stable identifier.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: The vocabulary duplicates standard principals, permissions, capabilities, policies, and delegation terminology without adding testable distinctions.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: Two materially different authority states map to the same declared term and decision, or a term has no unique consumer or failure consequence beyond existing security vocabulary.

required_lanes.necessity: Crosswalk terms to established security and governance definitions.
Justify retained differences and ownership.
Exercise each retained term in valid and invalid decisions.

acceptance_criteria: Every retained term has one canonical definition, serialization, consumer, and distinct failure consequence; redundant terms are merged with lineage.

assumptions: Information access and external effect authority are separately observable for the tested operation.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: Access to sensitive information necessarily implies some authorized use, so strict separation creates unusable or artificial states.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: A read or transform grant authorizes disclose, write, execute, or approve without an explicit transition, or a matched separation design cannot preserve useful read/transform work within the frozen refusal budget.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify non-implication between permission classes.
Attempt class-promotion attacks through real adapters and data flows.
Compare separated and collapsed classes at matched information access.
Measure unsafe promotion and useful-task refusal.

acceptance_criteria: All unauthorized class promotions fail before effect, while authorized read and transform work remains within the preregistered useful-throughput and false-refusal gates.

assumptions: Required authority and authorized defaults are declared before the request.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: Failing closed on missing authority produces excessive false refusal; a model or ambient session can safely infer permission for routine cases.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: A missing required authority record yields an effect rather than denial/escalation, or fail-closed behavior exceeds the frozen false-refusal/latency budget without reducing unsafe effects.

required_lanes.necessity: Define missing, defaulted, denied, and escalated states.
Inject absent, malformed, stale, conflicting, and authorized-default records.
Measure unsafe inference, false refusal, latency, and escalation burden.
Compare fail-closed, ambient-inference, and bounded-default policies.

acceptance_criteria: All missing required authority fails closed or uses a prospectively authorized bounded default, and the policy meets the exact unsafe-effect and useful-throughput gates.

assumptions: Tuple fields refer to one effect attempt and can be joined across planning, routing, execution, receipt, replay, and revocation.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: A single tuple is over-normalized; subsystem-local identifiers with a correlation map are more reliable and scalable.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: Two fields fork across subsystems without detection, a historical tuple authorizes a new epoch, a tuple field has no consumer, or tuple joining adds more inconsistency than a strong correlation-map comparator.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify identity continuity, scope narrowing, and epoch constraints.
Replay cross-system traces and mutate each identity field.
Measure join failure, overhead, and stale-replay detection against a strong alternative.

acceptance_criteria: Independent replay joins every stage to one tuple, all seeded forks and stale epochs are rejected, and join/latency overhead remains within the frozen budget.

assumptions: Domain and role owners can be authenticated independently of the requesting executor.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: Separate domain, budget, trace, and revocation owners create organizational overhead without improving enforcement over one trusted service owner.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: Cross-domain effects proceed without authenticated target-owner approval, ownership conflicts are unresolved, or separation adds no detection/resistance benefit in matched laundering attacks while exceeding the cost gate.

required_lanes.necessity: Define accountability and permitted co-location of owner roles.
Exercise source/target approval, denial, ownership conflict, and revocation.
Compare separated and co-located ownership under matched attacks.
Measure laundering detection, delay, and operator burden.

acceptance_criteria: Every cross-domain effect has authenticated target-owner approval, conflicts fail closed, and the chosen ownership topology meets the frozen security/usability gate.

assumptions: Protocol and hardware evidence lanes have prospectively defined artifacts and claim ceilings.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: Strong protocol authentication plus device-derived keys is sufficient practical evidence for a hardware-root claim in the intended system.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: The chapter cannot distinguish protocol authentication from secure boot, protected key custody, measured execution, tamper resistance, or attestation, or a protocol-only artifact promotes a hardware-root support effect.

required_lanes.necessity: Use current hardware-root definitions and threat models rather than local terminology.
Reject protocol-only records that assert hardware-root evidence.
Encode the no-implication boundary without claiming hardware truth.

acceptance_criteria: Every protocol artifact remains in its lane, hardware-root claims require their own accepted evidence, and all laundering mutations are rejected.

assumptions: Read, transform, disclose, write, execute, and approve cover the current effect surface; extensions require versioned taxonomy changes.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.strongest_counterclaim: The six classes overlap, omit important capabilities, and create false precision compared with operation-specific policies.
system-boundaries-and-authority.mechanis.falsifier: A material operation maps ambiguously to incompatible classes, a class promotion occurs without an explicit transition, or an operation-specific comparator enforces the same boundaries with lower complexity.

required_lanes.necessity: Compare the taxonomy with established access-control rights and information-flow operations.
Justify class boundaries and extension policy.
Check class non-implication and explicit promotion.
Mutation-test every pairwise prohibited promotion on representative adapters.

acceptance_criteria: Every tested operation has one prospective class or explicit compound policy, all prohibited promotions fail, and taxonomy residuals are recorded rather than forced into an incorrect class.

assumptions: Issuer and revocation authority identities are authenticated and not controlled solely by the executor receiving the grant.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.strongest_counterclaim: Versioned grant issuance can still be forged or stale and adds little beyond a policy decision API.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.falsifier: A ceiling or grant lacks issuer, version, scope, expiry, or revocation owner; an executor self-issues an unauthorized grant; or consumers accept an unknown/stale version.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify finite issuance, version, and lifecycle validity.
Test forged, self-issued, stale, conflicting, expired, and revoked grants.

acceptance_criteria: Only authenticated, current, scoped grants from the declared issuer reach consumers, and every invalid issuance class has an exact denial record.

assumptions: All declared effectful adapters are behind a mediation point that can check before mutation and observe afterward.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.strongest_counterclaim: Adapters can emit compliant receipts while bypass paths or TOCTOU changes allow unmediated effects.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.falsifier: An effect occurs before or without tuple admission, request/effect identity changes after check, or the emitted receipt disagrees with independent effect/no-effect observation.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify precondition, atomicity, and receipt linkage at the modeled boundary.
Attack mediation bypass, TOCTOU, receipt forgery, crash, and rollback paths.
Measure enforcement latency, bypass detection, and receipt accuracy.

acceptance_criteria: Every declared adapter effect is prechecked, atomically bound or compensably reconciled to the admitted tuple, and independently matched to one truthful effect or denial receipt.

assumptions: The evidence consumer is sufficiently independent to compare receipts with policy state and observed effects.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.strongest_counterclaim: Recording authority failures adds audit volume but does not improve detection or recovery because receipts share the executor's trust base.
system-boundaries-and-authority.interfac.falsifier: A seeded missing, inconsistent, expired, revoked, over-ceiling, or cross-domain violation is absent from evidence, misclassified, or cannot be traced to effect/no-effect and residual ownership.

required_lanes.necessity: Join issuer, consumer, receipt, independent observation, incident, and residual records.
Measure failure-detection recall, false alarm, delay, and recovery cost.
Ablate independent evidence checks and receipt joins.

acceptance_criteria: The evidence path detects every seeded material failure at the frozen rate, preserves exact lineage and owner, and does not promote support without an accepted transition.

assumptions: Authority states have a declared partial order and every widening edge requires an authenticated governance grant.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Authority dimensions are not totally comparable, and legitimate dynamic widening cannot always wait for explicit governance.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A reachable transition widens any declared authority dimension without an authenticated explicit grant or authorized emergency rule, or the order cannot represent a material permission change.

required_lanes.necessity: Define authority dimensions and emergency exception policy.
Check monotonicity and explicit widening edges.
Mutation-test every widening dimension and consumer.

acceptance_criteria: All ungranted widening mutations fail, authorized widening is explicit and versioned, and incomparable dimensions route to review rather than silent admission.

residual.next_unblocking_condition: Authenticated identity, trusted time, deployed complete mediation, concurrent distributed revocation, independent attacks, and transfer.

assumptions: Read and write operations are distinguishable at the mediated target.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Read operations can have side effects and write permissions can be implicit in mutable interfaces, so the binary distinction is inadequate.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A read grant permits target mutation or disclosure without explicit compound authority, or side-effecting reads are classified as pure read and admitted.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify read, write, disclose, and compound operation boundaries.
Probe side-effecting reads and read-to-write/disclose promotion.

acceptance_criteria: Pure read produces no unauthorized mutation/disclosure, side-effecting reads require compound authority, and all class-confusion controls are rejected.

assumptions: Every effectful tool call is mediated and the required grant can be resolved before invocation.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Sandbox defaults or low-impact allowlists can safely authorize tool execution without a per-call explicit grant.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A tool effect occurs with no explicit active grant or prospectively authorized bounded default, or the grant is checked only after mutation.

required_lanes.necessity: Define explicit grant and bounded-default semantics.
Attack missing, wrong-tool, wrong-target, expired, revoked, and post-effect grants.
Measure unsafe calls and false refusals against allowlist comparators.

acceptance_criteria: Every effectful call is preceded by an applicable active grant or declared bounded default, all invalid cases fail before mutation, and usability remains within the gate.

assumptions: Delegation and effect records share stable grant, budget, trace, replay, and epoch identities.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Delegation legitimately changes accounting or trace identities, so strict preservation is brittle and unnecessary if correlation remains possible.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: Delegation widens scope, forks or drops any required identity without an explicit migration record, or replay cannot reconstruct the complete chain and epoch.

required_lanes.necessity: Check scope subset and identity continuity relations.
Seed scope widening, identifier substitution, chain truncation, and stale replay.
Measure join completeness and overhead on multi-hop traces.

acceptance_criteria: Every delegation narrows or preserves scope, maintains or explicitly migrates all required identities, and replays to one complete grant/epoch chain.

assumptions: Target-domain identity and owner approval can be authenticated separately from source-domain possession.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Federated trust or preauthorized domain relationships can make per-handoff target-owner approval unnecessary.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A cross-domain effect occurs without target-owner approval or a prospectively scoped federation rule, or source-domain ambient capability substitutes for target consent.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify direct and federated approval semantics.
Attack missing, forged, stale, wrong-target, and overbroad federation approvals.

acceptance_criteria: Every cross-domain effect binds authenticated target consent or a valid scoped federation rule, and all ambient inheritance controls fail.

assumptions: Hardware-root claims have separately defined evidence requirements.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Authenticated protocol and device-derived key records are close enough to hardware-root evidence for the intended claim scope.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A protocol-only record causes hardware-root admission or the evidence model cannot distinguish protocol authenticity from secure boot, protected keys, measured execution, tamper resistance, and attestation.

required_lanes.necessity: Anchor the evidence distinction in primary hardware-root specifications.
Check no-implication in the finite evidence model.
Reject laundering controls across all claim consumers.

acceptance_criteria: No protocol artifact alone reaches a hardware-root state, and every hardware-root claim names its independent artifact and verifier.

assumptions: High-impact approval classes and executor identities are prospectively declared; any emergency self-approval rule is separately scoped and reviewed.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Independent approval is too slow and unnecessary for low-risk or high-confidence effects; executor self-approval can be bounded safely.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: An executor issues or controls the approval that authorizes its own covered effect without an explicit bounded exception, or nominal independence shares an undeclared controlling identity.

required_lanes.necessity: Define which effects require independence and acceptable emergency exceptions.
Specify issuer/executor separation and shared-control conflicts.
Attack self-approval, collusion identity, stale approval, and exception scope.
Measure unsafe approval, delay, fatigue, and false refusal.

acceptance_criteria: Every covered effect has independently controlled approval or a prospectively bounded exception, all self-approval controls fail, and approval burden meets the usability gate.

assumptions: Every downstream authority consumer can query a current lifecycle view or receive invalidation before effect.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.strongest_counterclaim: Strong consistency for lifecycle visibility is infeasible; local caching with eventual reconciliation is sufficient.
system-boundaries-and-authority.invarian.falsifier: A downstream consumer acts on an expired, revoked, over-depth, or denied grant without detecting its state before effect or invoking a prospectively bounded stale-state policy.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify consistency, stale-state, depth, denial, and bounded partition semantics.
Inject expiry, revocation, delegation, denial, delay, partition, and cache-staleness events.
Measure propagation delay, stale use, false refusal, and recovery.
Repeat across downstream consumers and storage/notification implementations.

acceptance_criteria: Every declared consumer observes lifecycle changes before covered effects or follows the exact bounded partition policy, with stale-use and false-refusal rates inside frozen limits.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: Repeated unrecorded widening, permissive defaults, or exception accumulation can make active authority exceed the reviewed ceiling.
assumptions: Active authority and reviewed ceilings can be independently reconstructed over time.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Operational authority changes are adequately controlled by ordinary configuration and review without explicit transition lineage.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Removing transition lineage and ceiling checks does not increase unreviewed widening or unsafe effects under matched change sequences, or the proposed audit cannot detect seeded creep.

required_lanes.necessity: Run multi-step grant, exception, replacement, and policy-change sequences.
Ablate transition lineage and ceiling enforcement.
Measure creep detection, unsafe effects, and operational burden.

acceptance_criteria: The governed path detects all seeded material widening and materially reduces unreviewed active authority without exceeding the frozen burden gate.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: A higher-authority tool can launder ambient power for a lower-authority requester when the operation is not bound to the requester's ceiling and grant.
assumptions: Requester authority, tool ambient authority, target effect, and mediation path are independently observable.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Tool wrappers and model policies already prevent confused-deputy behavior without per-request authority binding.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Under matched adversarial requests, removing requester-bound authority does not increase unauthorized tool effects, or the bound design fails to prevent the seeded laundering cases.

required_lanes.necessity: Use the original confused-deputy and current agent-tool security comparator definitions.
Attack live or sandboxed tools with lower-authority requesters and ambient-power deputies.
Ablate requester binding at matched tool capability.
Measure unauthorized effects, refusal, and latency.

acceptance_criteria: All preregistered laundering attacks fail before effect under requester binding, and the matched ambient control exhibits the claimed failure difference within the uncertainty rule.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: Treating memory or source access as disclosure, execution, or approval authority can convert available context into unauthorized effects.
assumptions: Information access and each downstream effect class are separately observable.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Trusted memory contents and user-provided sources can safely carry executable authority in the intended workflow.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Collapsing memory access into action authority does not increase unauthorized disclosure/execution/approval under matched adversarial content, or class separation cannot block seeded cases.

required_lanes.necessity: Inject instructions and secrets into accessible memory under absent action grants.
Compare separated and collapsed permission policies.
Measure unauthorized effects and useful retrieval loss.

acceptance_criteria: Accessible content never supplies undeclared action authority, seeded instruction/secret attacks fail, and legitimate retrieval remains within the useful-throughput gate.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: Forking grant, budget, trace, replay, or revocation identity can let subsystems accept locally valid records that no longer describe one authorized effect.
assumptions: The canonical effect identity and each subsystem key can be independently observed.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Correlation maps and local identifiers adequately join effect records; one tuple is unnecessary.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Seeded identity forks cause no unauthorized, unbudgeted, unauditable, or stale effect under the strong correlation-map control, or the unified tuple fails to detect them.

required_lanes.necessity: Fork each identity across multi-system traces.
Compare unified identity with strong correlation mapping.
Measure detection, join error, cost, and replay accuracy.

acceptance_criteria: The chosen identity scheme detects every seeded material fork and prevents the corresponding effect while meeting the join and overhead gates.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: A source-domain grant or historical receipt can be laundered into target-domain or current-epoch authority when target approval and revocation epoch are not rebound.
assumptions: Domain and epoch boundaries are prospectively declared and target approval can be authenticated.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Federated trust and replay protection already prevent these cases without per-handoff target approval and epoch rebinding.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Removing target approval or epoch rebinding does not increase cross-domain/stale effects under matched attacks, or the proposed design admits a seeded laundering/replay case.

required_lanes.necessity: Attack domain crossing and replay with valid source grants and stale receipts.
Ablate target consent and epoch binding separately.
Measure attack success, refusal, and propagation cost.

acceptance_criteria: All preregistered domain-laundering and stale-epoch attacks fail before effect, and legitimate federated handoffs meet the usability gate.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: Presenting protocol authentication, encryption, signatures, replay windows, or device-derived keys as hardware-root evidence overstates the trust boundary.
assumptions: Claim reviewers can distinguish the artifacts and support ceilings of protocol and hardware lanes.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: The practical security claim does not require a strict protocol/hardware distinction at the intended scope.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Removing the lane distinction does not increase independently adjudicated hardware-root overclaims, or the proposed classifier cannot detect seeded laundering.

required_lanes.necessity: Define hardware-root evidence from primary specifications.
Seed protocol-only artifacts with hardware-root claim attempts.
Compare typed and collapsed evidence lanes.

acceptance_criteria: All seeded protocol-to-hardware promotions are rejected and independently adjudicated overclaim falls by the frozen amount without erasing valid protocol claims.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: Delayed or missing lifecycle propagation can leave expired or revoked grants usable in downstream or delegated contexts.
assumptions: Expiry/revocation events and downstream uses have an independently observed order or bounded clock uncertainty.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Short-lived caches and eventual consistency make stale-grant effects negligible compared with the availability cost of strong propagation.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: Under realistic delay, partition, and cache scenarios, lifecycle propagation policy does not change stale-effect rate within the uncertainty rule, or the proposed policy exceeds availability limits without reducing material harm.

required_lanes.necessity: Specify bounded clock, partition, and stale-state semantics.
Run expiry/revocation races across delegated consumers.
Vary propagation and cache policies.
Measure stale effects, availability, latency, and recovery.
Repeat across storage and notification implementations.

acceptance_criteria: The selected policy meets exact stale-effect and availability gates across the declared delay/partition transfer matrix.

system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.proposition: A replacement implementation can inherit authority handles qualified only for its predecessor, bypassing requalification and changing the effective authority subject.
assumptions: Implementation identity and handle qualification are versioned and observable across replacement.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.strongest_counterclaim: Stable capability-field identity should preserve handles across replacement because the field contract, not implementation identity, owns authority.
system-boundaries-and-authority.failure_.falsifier: A replacement with changed implementation identity safely reuses predecessor handles across all declared risk classes without requalification, or the requalification policy cannot detect seeded incompatible replacements.

required_lanes.necessity: Define which authority belongs to the stable field and which to a qualified implementation.
Run compatible, incompatible, revoked, rollback, and stale-handle replacement sequences.
Compare automatic inheritance with identity-bound requalification.
Measure unsafe inheritance, downtime, and review cost.

acceptance_criteria: All implementation-bound handles require successful requalification, field-bound handles preserve only declared scope, seeded incompatible inheritance fails, and replacement burden meets the gate.

assumptions: The finite sequential model's numeric grant/principal/operation/target identities, receipt fields, epoch, expiry, source records, and observations faithfully represent the bounded event; authenticity, concurrency, complete mediation, and deployed enforcement are external assumptions.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem restates ceiling preservation in its record validity assumptions and provides no nontrivial guarantee about real effects or multidimensional authority.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.falsifier: A Lean countermodel satisfies the intended premises while authority widens without grant, a ceiling/grant field is semantically unused, or a runtime refinement disagrees on a reachable transition.

required_lanes.necessity: Check nonempty positive and widening countercases over the exact authority order.
Differentially test runtime transitions and mutate ceiling, grant, issuer, and authority dimensions.

acceptance_criteria: The kernel checks nontrivial reachable witnesses, every material field mutation reaches the expected result, multidimensional residuals are explicit, and all concrete consumers refine the modeled decision.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

assumptions: The finite sequential model's grant binding, approval, dispatch, receipt, epoch, expiry, use-count, revocation, and observation fields faithfully represent the bounded event; detection, authenticity, concurrency, and mediation are external assumptions.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.strongest_counterclaim: The theorem is a direct projection from a missing-grant premise to denial and proves no independent enforcement property.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.falsifier: A reachable modeled execution with missing grant is authorized, grant presence is unused, or a concrete consumer effects the corresponding invalid record.

required_lanes.necessity: Check reachable missing/present/invalid grant cases and dependency use.
Mutation-test grant state before independently observed effects.

acceptance_criteria: Missing and invalid grants are reachable and denied, valid grants reach only bounded authorization, and every effect consumer agrees under mutation.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

assumptions: The finite fields cover the declared lifecycle taxonomy but do not establish identity truth, clock truth, receipt faithfulness, complete mediation, or open-world exhaustiveness.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.strongest_counterclaim: The route theorem is field-completeness projection bloat that checks metadata presence without authorization semantics or real consumers.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.falsifier: A required field or failed state can be removed/corrupted without reaching its exact route, a complete finite record cannot reach bounded admission, or a concrete consumer disagrees with the formal outcome.

required_lanes.necessity: Check each exact lifecycle branch, precedence, nonempty admission, and unreachable-state assumptions.
Mutation-test every serialized field and consuming admission gate without claiming value truth.

acceptance_criteria: Every declared mutation reaches its exact outcome, valid records reach bounded admission, all branches have real consumers or are retired, and runtime decisions refine the finite route.

reproduction.evaluator_identity: Lean 4 kernel in the pinned Lake environment

assumptions: The finite summary accurately reflects separately replayed authority, adapter, SCIF, and reference-trace artifacts; Lean does not inspect files, clocks, effects, or deployed services.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.strongest_counterclaim: The bridge is a hard-coded conjunction of recorded booleans with no semantic value beyond the executable validators and should be retired as proof bloat.
system-boundaries-and-authority.formal.a.falsifier: The bridge accepts a missing blocked-authority or no-promotion field, disagrees with a replayed artifact, or has no independent consumer requiring the cross-artifact invariant.

required_lanes.necessity: Determine whether the bridge expresses a nontrivial cross-artifact invariant or should be retired with lineage.
Replay each source artifact, mutate each summary field, and bind the serialized result to the Lean fixture input.

acceptance_criteria: Every required field mutation is rejected by replay and Lean, artifact digests bind the summary, and P2 records a real consumer or retires the bridge without losing stronger executable checks.

reproduction.environment_lock: Python dependency lock plus lean/lake-manifest.json and lean/lean-toolchain
reproduction.evaluator_identity: Python authority-trace validator and Lean 4 kernel in pinned repository environments

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.e5.rationale: Plain-language causal motivation for the no-ambient-authority core boundary.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.76.rationale: Examples of competence and context failing to create external-effect authority.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.60.rationale: Reader-language restatement of downstream denial and revocation visibility.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.01.rationale: Applies the no-ambient-authority core to efficient route selection.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.16.rationale: SCF-specific example of predecessor-handle inheritance across replacement.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.4b.rationale: Restates that capability, context, route quality, source access, and enthusiasm do not confer authority.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.99.rationale: Plain-language requirement that missing authority becomes denial or escalation.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.c8.rationale: Illustrates fail-closed authority despite coherent plans and capable models.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.44.rationale: Reader-path emphasis on the boundary already owned by the core.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.13.rationale: Correct evidence boundary that external comparators do not prove local enforcement.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.d7.rationale: Transition into the replacement and self-improvement failure examples.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.82.rationale: Source-mapping and current support-state limitation, not a new authority claim.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.0b.rationale: Figure walkthrough of pre-effect tuple validation.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.6d.rationale: VIEA source-synthesis introduction to authority ownership.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.69.rationale: Restates target-domain approval and no ambient inheritance.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.24.rationale: Diagram explanation of unified identity continuity.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.a7.rationale: Cross-domain refusal and revoked-epoch replay instance.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.bd.rationale: Explicit list of hardware-root properties not proved by protocol controls.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.90.rationale: States the central enforcement objection without adding a positive proposition.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.f3.rationale: Exact limitation of the tuple fixture and list of residual trust roots.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.62.rationale: Enumerates authority-transition fields already owned by the minimum artifact.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.fb.rationale: Correct non-claim that field presence is not enforcement.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.42.rationale: Table introduction for the six manifest-owned permission classes.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.62.rationale: Expanded target-domain approval invariant with ambient-process noninheritance.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.d7.rationale: Literal prose form of downstream lifecycle visibility.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.b2.rationale: Object-capability lineage and confused-deputy discipline, with an explicit non-implementation boundary.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.11.rationale: Subset of the unified authority tuple fields.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.de.rationale: Finite field list and scope statement for the minimum artifact.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.51.rationale: Concrete missing-grant and over-ceiling denial behavior.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.93.rationale: Denial reason preservation for downstream consumers.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.40.rationale: Summary of the first three manifest invariants.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.a1.rationale: Endpoint disposition summary for owned authority failure modes.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.d8.rationale: Mature-deployment form of downstream denial visibility.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.ff.rationale: Current support ceiling and exact evidence required before stronger authority claims.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.c4.rationale: Handoff sentence into the failure-radar chapter rather than a new authority claim.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.61.rationale: Reports the bounded reachable-model and executed-consumer result already owned by the strengthened Authority operational-invariant target.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.2d.rationale: Explicit non-claim boundary for the finite Authority proof packet; it adds no positive proposition.

system-boundaries-and-authority.prose.ef.rationale: Line-wrapped report of the bounded six-event witness and 38 rejecting mutations; its limitations remain explicit and it adds no broader authority claim.

non_claims: Completing this semantic sweep does not establish enforcement, security, safety, live revocation, trusted identity or time, complete mediation, hardware roots, or support promotion.
The five newly manifest-owned obligations and narrowed core are claim repairs, not positive evidence.
Duplicate and source-report dispositions preserve canonical ownership and evidence boundaries; they do not prove the target atoms or source claims.
